rulings.Case Title: NEW LIVABLE CALIFORNIA ET AL VS. ASSOCIATION OF BAY AREA GOVERNMENTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent Association Of Bay Area Governments' Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification. (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 2, Respondent Association Of Bay Area Governments' Demurrer To Petition For Writ Of Mandate/ Prohibition/ Certification(Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling Only) Furthermore, the statement by ABAG in its announcement of the President's Report on May 16, 2019 that it will conduct roll-call votes for all nonunanimous actions, of which the Court takes judicial notice, neutralizes the controversy moving forward and renders the request for mandate and declaratory relief superfluous. (RJN; see California High-Speed Rail Authority v. Superior Court (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 676, 684 ["writ of mandamus will not lie to compel the idle act of rescinding and redoing" a funding plan where the plan had already been acted upon by Legislature in appropriating bond funds]; County of San Diego v. State of California (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 580, 595-596 ["As a general proposition courts will not issue a writ of mandate to enforce an abstract right of no practical benefit to petitioner, or where to issue the writ would be useless, unenforceable, or unavailing."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the respondent is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: NEW LIVABLE CALIFORNIA ET AL VS. ASSOCIATION OF BAY AREA GOVERNMENTS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 2, RESPONDENT ASSOCIATION OF BAY AREA GOVERNMENTS' DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION.(part 1 of 2) Respondent Association of Bay Area Governments' (ABAG) demurrer to Petitioners New Livable California and Community Venture Partners, Inc.'s petition for writ of mandate and complaint for injunctive and declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend. Government Code section 54953 requires that teleconferenced meetings such as the one at issue in this case have all votes taken by roll call, but Petitioners admit that the Amended CASA Motion challenged in their Petition was adopted by a valid, roll-call vote on January 18, 2019. (Pet. 14, 27.) The plain text of the Ralph M. Brown Act allows for invalidation of an individual vote if that vote violated the statute. (See Gov. Code § 54960.1(a) [a court is authorized to declare null and void "an action taken by a legislative body of a local agency in violation of Section 54953"] [emphasis added]; Gov. Code § 54952.6 ["action taken" defined as "a collective decision," "a collective commitment," or "an actual vote"].) There is no statutory basis for invalidating a subsequent action taken by ABAG based on an alleged prior violation of the Brown Act on a procedural motion such as the Substitute Motion involved here, nor do Petitioners do not cite any authority that supports their position. In addition, ABAG is not required to adhere to the Rules of Order cited by Petitioners and their failure to do so does not render its actions arbitrary as a matter of law. Therefore, Petitioners cannot state a cause of action as to the Amended CASA Motion. Petitioners are also unable to state a claim as to the Substitute Motion, which would have continued the Amended CASA Motion to a later date if it had passed. While Olson v. Hornbrook Community Services Dist. (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 502, 522 suggests that Petitioners are not required to allege prejudice to state a Brown Act claim, Petitioners have not established any possibility of demonstrating legally cognizable prejudice on these facts. Under such circumstances, where "no prejudice is apparent," the Court has jurisdiction to sustain the demurrer. (See Galbiso v. Orosi Public Utility Dist. (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 652, 671 [sustaining demurrer without leave to amend because no prejudice was apparent from purported Brown Act violation.) Petitioners' allegations of prejudice are based on their right to monitor and report on each director's individual vote and their desire to see the Substitute Motion pass. (Pet. 36-37, 76-78.) Neither is a meaningful source of prejudice, especially given that the Substitute Motion failed by a vote of 18 to 9 and there is no basis to allege that any directors who voted against the Substitute Motion would have changed their vote if voting by roll call when they were willing to have their roll-call votes in favor of the Amended CASA Motion publicly reported. (Pet. 22, 27.) (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Against Gatti
rulings.Rulings: August 12, 2019 Line 1, DEFENDANT LOOP AI LABS INC., GIANMAURO CALAFIORE Motion To Compel Discovery Against Gatti Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to compel will be granted. Plaintiff's contention that the motion is untimely is based on: an unsigned (and therefore unsworn) declaration; a declaration which does not demonstrate personal knowledge on the part of the declarant; and otherwise fragmentary and/or contested evidence. The Court will not deny the motion to compel based on this showing. Plaintiff is therefore ordered to provide complete and verified responses to the form and special interrogatories and the request for production of documents identified in defendants' moving papers and to produce all documents identified in response to said request, all within 30 days of date of order. Plaintiff's objections to said discovery requests, except as to privilege, are overruled. Plaintiff shall submit a code-compliant privilege log as to any information or documents withheld from production on grounds of privilege within 30 days of the date of order. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. In addition, the prevailing party shall transmit a copy of the proposed order in Word format to the Pro Tem at the email address set forth above. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: MIGUEL ENRIQUE LOPEZ VS. MARI A KAWAGUCHI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Compulsory Cross-Complaint And/Or Reinstate Previously Dismissed Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 6, DEFENDANT MARI KAWAGUCHI's Motion For Leave To File A Compulsory Cross-Complaint And/Or Reinstate Previously Dismissed Cross-Complaint. Defendant Mari A. Kawaguchi's motion for leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint is granted. (Code Civ. Proc. §§ 426.50, 428.50(c); Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99 [even on "eve of trial," leave to file compulsory cross-complaint is mandatory absent bad faith].) Plaintiff has not shown that Defendant is acting in bad faith or has unduly delayed to his prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT STANG ET AL VS. BRY 1890, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Discovery Misuse
rulings.Rulings: August 12, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANTS BRY1890 LLC,, MRY101 LLC'S, Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Discovery Misuse Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs Robert Stang and Luigi Di Ruocco have committed numerous and continuous misuses of the discovery process as defined in Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.010(b), (d), (f), and (g), including disobedience to a specific discovery order of this Court. Without any satisfactory explanation or excuse, plaintiffs continued their discovery abuses and disobedience until a few days before the hearing on this motion when they at long last served discovery responses. However, said responses were defective and non-code-compliant in multiple respects. Moreover, there is no evidence that plaintiffs have paid the monetary sanctions previously awarded against them. Accordingly, defendants' motion will be granted to the following extent: 1. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2023.030(d)(1), plaintiffs' complaint filed October 19, 2018 is dismissed with prejudice as a terminating sanction for misuses of the discovery process; 2. Within 30 days of the date of order hereon, plaintiffs shall file code-compliant discovery responses, without objection, to all of defendants' discovery requests except those specifically pertaining to plaintiffs' complaint filed October 19, 2018; 3. Within 30 days of the date of order hereon, plaintiffs and their counsel shall pay all sanctions previously awarded against them and shall further pay to defendants the additional sum of $2655 representing attorney's fees incurred by defendants in making this motion. Defendants' motion is otherwise denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. In addition, the prevailing party shall transmit a copy of the proposed order in Word format to the Pro Tem at the email address set forth above. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP RASKIN VS. MARK LUTZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Documents And Further Responses To Plaintiffs First Requests For Documents From Defendant North Bay Mpc Llc
rulings.Rulings: August 12, 2019, Line 4, PLAINTIFF PHILIP RASKIN'S Motion To Compel Documents And Further Responses To Plaintiffs First Requests For Documents From Defendant North Bay Mpc Llc Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 170.1 and Canon E of the Code of Judicial Ethics, the Pro Tem Judge discloses that he was previously associated in the private practice of law with one of plaintiff's attorneys, Eric G. Wallis, but such association ended more than two years prior to the assignment of the Pro Tem Judge to this matter and that the actual acquaintanceship of Mr. Wallis and the Pro Tem Judge was slight. Accordingly, the Pro Tem Judge believes there is no actual basis for his disqualification present. The motion will be granted to the extent that defendant shall produce all requested lease documents within ten days after the date of order hereon. The motion will otherwise be denied because defendant has responded to plaintiff's request for production of documents and produced documents as maintained in the regular course of business. The manner of response to the request and the method of production are authorized by California law. California law does not impose any further requirement to segregate, label or index documents so produced. Kayne v. The Grande Holdings Ltd., 198 Cal.App.4th 1470 (2011), is not to the contrary as to documents produced as maintained in the regular course of business. This ruling is without prejudice to plaintiff's right to seek further production of items claimed to have been omitted from defendant's production of documents to date. The parties are urged to resolve such matters informally and without court intervention. Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. In addition, the prevailing party shall transmit a copy of the proposed order in Word format to the Pro Tem at the email address set forth above. (JPT/EPS/302).

rulings.Case Title: GARY STAPLETON VS. BURLINGAME POINT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT AZUL WORKS HOLDING INC.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint. Defendant Azul Works Holding Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff Gary Stapleton's first amended complaint is granted in part and denied in part. The allegations related to Mr. Debarro's apology, as pleading evidentiary facts, and the improper citation to a superseded Court of Appeal opinion, are stricken from the pleadings as improper matter, as plaintiff concedes. The motion is also granted with respect to the allegations of cross-agency and employment, because these boilerplate allegations are factually inconsistent with other allegations in the complaint. The ruling is without prejudice to plaintiff seeking leave to amend to add allegations as to the relationship among the various defendants after conducting discovery. The motion is denied as to the request to strike the negligence per se cause of action, and is also denied as to the conclusory allegations of negligence, which are sufficiently supported by factual allegations. (See Perkins v. Superior Court (1981) 117 Cal.App.3d 1, 6 ["the courts have permitted allegations which obviously included conclusions of law and have termed them 'ultimate facts' or 'conclusions of facts.' " [Citations]. What is important is that the complaint as a whole contain sufficient facts to apprise the defendant of the basis upon which the plaintiff is seeking relief."].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH ALLEN WHITE, II VS. SMULE, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 11, DEFENDANT SMULE, INC.'s Motion To Strike. Defendant Smule, Inc.'s motion to strike Plaintiff Kenneth Allen White, II's complaint is off calendar for failure to comply with statutory meet and confer requirements. Code of Civil Procedure section 435.5(a) provides that before filing a motion to strike, the moving party "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone" with the party who filed the pleading that is subject to the motion to strike with the purpose of attempting to "resolve[] the objections to be raised in the motion to strike." The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately. Defendant has until September 9, 2019 to respond to the complaint. Both parties are reminded that Local Rule 2.7(B) requires that courtesy copies of any papers filed with the court must be delivered to Department 302. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DEMINKA SPEARS VS. MOUNT ST. JOSEPH-ST. ELIZABETH DBA THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 12, 2019, Line 14, DEFENDANT MOUNT ST. JOSEPH-ST. ELIZABETH's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant Mount St. Joseph-St. Elizabeth dba The Epiphany Center's motion to strike is granted. The court strikes the claim for punitive damages pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 425.14. Plaintiff must obtain a court order before seeking that relief from defendant. Code of Civ. Proc., Sec. 425.14 provides that a plaintiff must obtain a court order before seeking punitive damages against a "religious corporation." The court takes judicial notice of defendant's Articles of Incorporation and Bylaws. Those documents, which plaintiff agrees the court can take judicial notice of, indisputably show that defendant is a religious corporation subject to the protections of section 425.14. Section 1 of the Bylaws states that the philosophy of the corporation is "in accordance with the official teachings of the Roman Catholic Church." Section 3 of the Bylaws states that "[t]his corporation is formed for the purpose of carrying out the mission of the DOC which is to imitate the love of Jesus Christ…" The court rejects plaintiff's argument that the statute does not apply because defendant is a "charitable nonprofit corporation" or public benefit corporation under Corporations Code Sec. 5110 et seq. and not a "religious corporation" under Corporations Code Sec. 9110 et seq. Defendant was incorporated in 1976 and at that time it was a religious corporation or organization. The Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law (Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 5110 et seq.) and Nonprofit Religious Corporation Law (Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 9110 et seq.) went into effect in 1980. Cal. Corp. Code, Sec. 5003(b) states that: The existence of corporations formed or existing on the date of enactment or reenactment of this part, Part 2, Part 3, Part 4 or Part 5 shall not be affected by the enactment or reenactment of such parts or by any change in the requirements for the formation of corporations or by the amendment or repeal of the laws under which they were formed or created. Plaintiff basically argues that defendant needed to reclassify itself in response to the new laws, but that is inconsistent with section 5003(b). (See also Cal. Corp. Code § 9912(a)(2) [any corporation organized primarily for religious purposes under prior law shall be subject to the new religious corporation law].) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
